brief_document.title: Deterministic time and random NIFs
brief_document.purpose: Implement the deterministic now/0, random/0, and random_int/2 NIFs that workflow code uses for time-dependent and randomised branching. These are pure functions that read from recorded history and deterministic seeds (D3) — they never touch a wall clock or OS entropy source. On replay, they produce identical values to first execution, which is the core determinism guarantee (CO3).
brief_document.task: Create nif_determinism.rs with production NIF implementations for now/0, random/0, and random_int/2. now/0 returns the recorded_at timestamp of the last event in the workflow's history via the DeterminismContext (or Resolver cursor). random/0 returns a deterministic float seeded from WorkflowId + RunId + the current command sequence position. random_int/2 returns a deterministic integer in [min, max] using the same seed. Replace the stubs in engine_nifs.rs with calls to these implementations.

requirements.title: now/0 returns last recorded event timestamp
requirements.spec: WHEN now/0 is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the recorded_at timestamp of the last event consumed by the Resolver's history cursor (D3, CO3). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT read the wall clock.
acceptance: nif_determinism.rs implements now_impl that constructs a NifContext and reads the last consumed event's recorded_at timestamp from the DeterminismContext or Resolver cursor.
The returned value is an integer (milliseconds since epoch) or an ISO-8601 binary — matching the aion_flow SDK's expected term format.
A test pre-populates a history with events at known timestamps, constructs a NifContext, calls now_impl, and asserts the returned timestamp matches the last event's recorded_at.
No std::time::SystemTime, chrono::Utc::now(), or other wall-clock call appears in the now path.

requirements.title: random/0 returns deterministic float
requirements.spec: WHEN random/0 is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL return a deterministic f64 in [0.0, 1.0) seeded from WorkflowId + RunId + the current sequence position (D3, CO3). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use any OS entropy source.
acceptance: nif_determinism.rs implements random_impl that derives a seed from workflow_id bytes + run_id bytes + the sequence position counter, hashes or feeds them into a deterministic PRNG, and returns an f64 in [0.0, 1.0).
The NIF returns the float as a BEAM float term.
A test calls random_impl twice with the same workflow_id, run_id, and sequence position and asserts the same value; a test with a different sequence position asserts a different value.
No rand::thread_rng(), OsRng, or getrandom call appears in the random path.

requirements.title: random_int/2 returns deterministic integer in range
requirements.spec: WHEN random_int/2 is called with min and max arguments, THE SYSTEM SHALL return a deterministic integer in [min, max] seeded from WorkflowId + RunId + the current sequence position (D3, CO3). IF min > max, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return an error term.
acceptance: nif_determinism.rs implements random_int_impl that takes min: i64 and max: i64, derives a seed identically to random/0, and maps the output to [min, max].
The NIF returns the integer as a BEAM integer term.
A test calls random_int_impl with min=1, max=100 twice at the same position and asserts the same value; a test with min > max asserts an error term.
The mapping from seed to integer range is uniform and deterministic.

requirements.title: Replay produces identical values
requirements.spec: WHEN now/0, random/0, or random_int/2 are called during replay, THE SYSTEM SHALL produce the same values as the original execution (C13, CO3). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT introduce any non-deterministic input that could vary between executions.
acceptance: A test runs a sequence of now, random, and random_int calls against a fresh history, records the returned values, then replays the same sequence over the recorded history and asserts all values match.
The seed derivation uses only workflow_id, run_id, and sequence position — no wall clock, no thread id, no random salt.
The same DeterminismContext (or cursor) advances consistently during both first execution and replay.

requirements.title: engine_nifs.rs wiring
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL replace the now/0, random/0, and random_int/2 stub entries in engine_nifs.rs with calls to nif_determinism.rs implementations.
acceptance: The NIF functions registered in engine_nif_entries() for now/0, random/0, and random_int/2 delegate to nif_determinism implementations.
now/0 is registered as a normal (non-dirty) NIF since it performs no I/O.
random/0 and random_int/2 are registered as normal NIFs since they are pure computations.
The stub {error, not_yet_implemented} responses are removed for these three NIFs.

boundaries: SHALL NOT read the wall clock or any OS entropy source from any code path in this brief (CO3).
SHALL NOT record any events — these are pure read-only functions, not command-issued side effects.
SHALL NOT depend on any AT or AD service — these NIFs read from the Resolver's history cursor and deterministic seeds only (D3).
SHALL NOT change the Resolver, HistoryCursor, or DeterminismContext types — consume them as-is.
verification: now/0 returns last recorded timestamp, not wall clock.
random/0 and random_int/2 produce identical values for identical (workflow_id, run_id, sequence_position) inputs.
Replay produces the same values as first execution.

checklist.text: now/0 returns the recorded_at timestamp of the last event in the workflow's history.
random/0 returns a deterministic float seeded from WorkflowId + RunId + sequence position.
random_int/2 returns a deterministic integer in the given range seeded from WorkflowId + RunId + sequence position.
now, random, and random_int produce identical values on replay as on first execution.

stories.text: As a workflow author, I want workflow.now to return a deterministic timestamp so that my workflow logic produces the same decisions on replay as on first execution.
As a workflow author, I want workflow.random to return a deterministic value so that randomised branching in my workflow replays correctly.

constraints.text: Every NIF goes through resolve_or_execute_live (or its equivalent replay check). No NIF may execute a side effect without first checking recorded history.
All event recording goes through the workflow's single-writer Recorder. No NIF calls EventStore::append directly.
Deterministic execution: workflow.now returns recorded timestamp, workflow.random is seeded from WorkflowId+RunId. No wall clock or entropy source in NIF-visible paths.
The aion crate remains transport-agnostic. Remote worker dispatch stays in aion-server's WorkerActivityDispatcher; the NIF bridge calls the ActivityDispatcher trait, not the concrete server type.
unsafe_code = deny. No unsafe in any new code.
Only the runtime module imports beamr. NIF bridge code uses RuntimeHandle, not beamr directly.
Tests run against InMemoryStore. No test requires a database or live server.

resolved_context.intention: Connect the last mile: the BEAM process calls aion_flow_ffi NIFs, and those NIFs go through the durability handoff so every side effect is recorded, replayed on recovery, and deterministic. When this cluster lands, a workflow that starts, runs activities on remote workers, sleeps, receives signals, spawns children, and completes produces a full, replayable event history — the Temporal developer expectation, not just for basic cases, but for all of them.
